workflowEn.label: Gather materials
workflowEn.detail: Prepare the lesson plan, template, approved images, and accessibility requirements.

workflowEn.label: Set the sequence
workflowEn.detail: Define the lesson opener, content, examples, practice, and desired Exit Ticket.

workflowEn.label: Create slides
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT create an editable slide deck with speaker notes and citations.

workflowEn.label: Review and refine
workflowEn.detail: Open the deck and improve its readability, teaching pace, and alignment with the plan.

checkEn: Have the instructor verify accuracy, learning sequence, accessibility, and image rights before teaching.

workflowEn.label: Gather the history
workflowEn.detail: Import stage history, call notes, emails, and any security, legal, or procurement issues.

workflowEn.label: Build a timeline
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT order the events and separate facts from assumptions.

workflowEn.label: Identify blockers
workflowEn.detail: List likely causes, supporting evidence, prior attempts, and involved stakeholders.

workflowEn.label: Plan deal recovery
workflowEn.detail: Define the next action, owner, conditions, and a draft customer message.

checkEn: Have the deal owner validate the evidence and approve every message before customer contact or CRM changes.

workflowEn.label: Frame the question
workflowEn.detail: State the initiative, target outcome, measurement period, and decision to be made.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the data
workflowEn.detail: Attach the experiment plan, metric definitions, cohort data, dashboard, and launch notes.

workflowEn.label: Analyze results
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT calculate lift, check guardrails, and compare differences across segments.

workflowEn.label: Create the readout
workflowEn.detail: Build an impact readout with charts, methods, limitations, and a recommendation.

checkEn: Have an analyst or experiment owner verify the figures, calculations, and confidence level before deciding.

workflowEn.label: Define the options
workflowEn.detail: State the decision, available options, time horizon, and key comparison criteria.

workflowEn.label: Gather inputs
workflowEn.detail: Attach the financial model, KPIs, plans, market context, and stakeholder constraints.

workflowEn.label: Model scenarios
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT compare each option's cost, timing, risk, ownership, and impact.

workflowEn.label: Test assumptions
workflowEn.detail: Change key variables to find the points where the recommendation would change.

checkEn: Have the data owners confirm every assumption and cost before presenting the comparison in a meeting.

workflowEn.label: Identify core files
workflowEn.detail: Mark which files are authoritative, which are drafts, and which must not be moved.

workflowEn.label: Inventory the folder
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT flag duplicates, unclear names, and outdated versions.

workflowEn.label: Propose a filing plan
workflowEn.detail: Design subfolders, naming rules, an Archive, and a README before making changes.

workflowEn.label: Organize and verify
workflowEn.detail: After approval, move or rename files, check links, and summarize every change.

checkEn: Confirm no files were deleted, links still work, and current versions remain easy to find after organizing.

workflowEn.label: Gather official materials
workflowEn.detail: Collect each course's syllabus, assignments, readings, calendar, and existing folders.

workflowEn.label: Audit and separate
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT separate official documents, personal notes, work in progress, and submitted work.

workflowEn.label: Create the structure
workflowEn.detail: Propose one folder per course, standard file names, a course index, and an Archive.

workflowEn.label: Build a tracker
workflowEn.detail: Extract deadlines from official sources with links and flag conflicting dates.

checkEn: Verify every deadline against official documents, and do not choose a date when sources conflict.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the inputs
workflowEn.detail: Gather calendars, work shifts, commitments, income, expenses, and bills for the planning period.

workflowEn.label: Set constraints
workflowEn.detail: Specify the currency, pay cycle, and which commitments or expenses are fixed or flexible.

workflowEn.label: Build the dashboard
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT show time conflicts, workload clusters, upcoming bills, and flexible spending.

workflowEn.label: Compare options
workflowEn.detail: Test changes to flexible time or spending to find a workable plan for the next week.

checkEn: Verify amounts and due dates at the source, and remove account numbers or unnecessary financial details before uploading.

workflowEn.label: Frame the decision
workflowEn.detail: State the question, governing rules, evaluation criteria, decision owner, and deadline.

workflowEn.label: Gather evidence
workflowEn.detail: Attach prior minutes, policy drafts, feedback, and program data, noting each source's status.

workflowEn.label: Assemble the packet
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT summarize evidence, options, tradeoffs, open issues, and the agenda.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the minutes
workflowEn.detail: Create a minutes template that clearly captures decisions, owners, and follow-up items.

checkEn: Verify citations, governing rules, confidential data, and fair treatment of differing views before distribution.

workflowEn.label: Prepare the model
workflowEn.detail: Attach the operating model, latest actuals, approved assumptions, owner inputs, and prior forecast.

workflowEn.label: Set the rules
workflowEn.detail: Identify changeable drivers, fixed assumptions, and definitions for base, upside, and downside.

workflowEn.label: Update scenarios
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT update a separate model copy and make every assumption change visible.

workflowEn.label: Assess the impact
workflowEn.detail: Compare revenue, margin, hiring, cash, sensitivity, and trigger points across scenarios.

checkEn: Have the plan owner verify formulas and data links, then approve every changed assumption before sharing the forecast.

workflowEn.label: Gather account signals
workflowEn.detail: Attach account records, calls, email threads, usage notes, and the previous plan.

workflowEn.label: Check freshness
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT flag stale data, changed stakeholders, discovery gaps, and open risks.

workflowEn.label: Refresh the strategy
workflowEn.detail: Build a stakeholder map, value hypothesis, objections, and proof points from the evidence.

workflowEn.label: Set next moves
workflowEn.detail: Tie each next-best action to supporting evidence, an owner, and a time frame.

checkEn: Have the account owner confirm the data and customer-safe content before updating CRM or sharing externally.

workflowEn.label: Set sources of truth
workflowEn.detail: Gather the syllabus, outcomes, slides, readings, assignments, rubrics, and calendar; mark official versions.

workflowEn.label: Audit the course
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT map learning outcomes to lessons, assessments, and weekly workload.

workflowEn.label: Prioritize updates
workflowEn.detail: Flag outdated, duplicate, or misaligned content with evidence, impact, and effort.

workflowEn.label: Revise in batches
workflowEn.detail: After instructor approval, update files in groups while preserving originals and a change summary.

checkEn: Have the instructor verify sources, policies, accessibility, and every recommendation requiring academic judgment.

workflowEn.label: Sort the feedback
workflowEn.detail: Gather feedback, the approved file list, and any points needing confirmation before edits.

workflowEn.label: Plan by file
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT map each feedback item to the affected file and content section.

workflowEn.label: Revise together
workflowEn.detail: After approval, update the deck, guide, handout, and answer key in one coordinated pass.

workflowEn.label: Check consistency
workflowEn.detail: Compare instructions, examples, answers, timing, and sources, then create a change log.

checkEn: Have the instructor confirm that examples, instructions, and answers match across all files before using the new version.

workflowEn.label: Define the evidence set
workflowEn.detail: Gather approved papers, notes, methods, prior proposals, and funder guidance.

workflowEn.label: Audit the inventory
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT identify missing files, duplicates, and sources that cannot be opened.

workflowEn.label: Synthesize themes
workflowEn.detail: Organize evidence by theme, disagreement, methods limitations, research aims, and open questions.

workflowEn.label: Build an evidence table
workflowEn.detail: Link each substantive claim to its source and flag points needing faculty confirmation.

checkEn: Verify every citation against the original and preserve the study scope, population, methods, and uncertainty.

workflowEn.label: Prepare de-identified data
workflowEn.detail: Use only approved, de-identified exports with metric definitions and a reporting period.

workflowEn.label: Define the joins
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT show how LMS, attendance, gradebook, and assignment data will join before charting.

workflowEn.label: Build the dashboard
workflowEn.detail: Show engagement trends, completion gaps, assessment patterns, and data-quality warnings.

workflowEn.label: Create a review queue
workflowEn.detail: Draft questions for faculty review instead of diagnosing students or automating outreach.

checkEn: Reconcile totals and spot-check source records; confirm date range, section, and metric definitions before use.

workflowEn.label: Gather your evidence
workflowEn.detail: Prepare your resume, portfolio, and verified experience notes after removing sensitive details.

workflowEn.label: Analyze each role
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT separate core and preferred requirements, evidence of fit, and gaps.

workflowEn.label: Build the tracker
workflowEn.detail: Create one row per role with its deadline, status, next action, and source link.

workflowEn.label: Draft and prepare
workflowEn.detail: Create a folder per role and draft a resume, cover letter, and interview questions for review.

checkEn: Verify every resume statement and interview answer against your real experience before using or submitting it.

workflowEn.label: Set the scope
workflowEn.detail: Choose one screen file and note the behavior, layout, navigation, and business rules to preserve.

workflowEn.label: Analyze the structure
workflowEn.detail: Have Codex find sections suited to child Views and side effects mixed into the body.

workflowEn.label: Extract and manage state
workflowEn.detail: Use clear inputs, @Binding, and callbacks; move logic to the right service or model.

workflowEn.label: Validate each stage
workflowEn.detail: Run a build, preview, or focused test after each significant extraction.

checkEn: Review the diff and build or test results to confirm business logic, navigation, analytics, and appearance are unchanged.

workflowEn.label: Define the users
workflowEn.detail: State the target audience, the site's main job, available content, and success criteria.

workflowEn.label: Limit the first version
workflowEn.detail: Choose only the pages and interactions required for the user's main journey.

workflowEn.label: Build and test
workflowEn.detail: Have Sites build a responsive site, then test navigation, links, forms, and keyboard access.

workflowEn.label: Prepare to publish
workflowEn.detail: Create a checklist for missing content, rights, privacy, accessibility, and approvals.

checkEn: Have the project owner verify content, links, image rights, privacy, and form-data destinations before publishing.

workflowEn.label: Define the exam scope
workflowEn.detail: Gather learning objectives, instructor guidance, notes, readings, exercises, and past quizzes.

workflowEn.label: Assess your time
workflowEn.detail: State the exam date, available study time, and topics where you lack confidence.

workflowEn.label: Build the practice system
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT create a concept map, study guide, spaced-practice calendar, and question bank.

workflowEn.label: Adapt to results
workflowEn.detail: Update the tracker after each practice session and adjust the next round to address gaps.

checkEn: Verify exam scope, questions, and answers against official materials, and flag topics the instructor has not confirmed.

workflowEn.label: Identify the sources
workflowEn.detail: Point to the Linear project, Slack thread, Drive, or Notion content to use as evidence.

workflowEn.label: Define the PRD structure
workflowEn.detail: List required sections such as problem, users, requirements, metrics, launch, and risks.

workflowEn.label: Draft with citations
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT use $documents to create the PRD with requirement-level citations.

workflowEn.label: Close the gaps
workflowEn.detail: Review the source appendix, conflicts, open questions, and claims lacking evidence.

checkEn: Have the PM and subject owners confirm requirements, decisions, and sources before sharing or updating work systems.

workflowEn.label: Define the outcome
workflowEn.detail: Set the objective, boundaries that must not change, and verifiable completion criteria.

workflowEn.label: Point to context
workflowEn.detail: List the files, issue, documents, logs, or PLAN.md that Codex must read first.

workflowEn.label: Set checkpoints
workflowEn.detail: Define commands or artifacts that measure progress at each checkpoint.

workflowEn.label: Track the goal
workflowEn.detail: Use /goal to view status and pause, resume, or clear the goal when needed.

checkEn: Set a clear stopping condition and test evidence before starting, and review status when scope or risk changes.

workflowEn.label: Choose one behavior
workflowEn.detail: Define one user promise, such as answer quality, grounding, or tool-call correctness.

workflowEn.label: Trace the real path
workflowEn.detail: Have Codex inspect the app path, prompts, adapters, and existing tests before proposing an eval plan.

workflowEn.label: Build the test set
workflowEn.detail: Add a Promptfoo config, sanitized fixtures, assertions, and a local run command.

workflowEn.label: Run a baseline
workflowEn.detail: Run the eval before changing behavior, then report passes, failures, and next tests.

checkEn: Ensure fixtures contain no sensitive data, assertions test real behavior, and product failures are not hidden by relaxed thresholds.

workflowEn.label: Gather user voices
workflowEn.detail: Point to Slack, Linear, Drive, and research notes that explain the problem and context.

workflowEn.label: Synthesize the story
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT turn the evidence into a user story, needs, constraints, and success criteria.

workflowEn.label: Explore designs
workflowEn.detail: Create several mock directions with ImageGen using the design system and existing information structure.

workflowEn.label: Build a prototype
workflowEn.detail: Choose a reviewed mock and have Codex turn it into a working prototype.

checkEn: Have users and the design team confirm the mock reflects the real problem and follows the design system before development.

workflowEn.label: Choose the core task
workflowEn.detail: Identify the user, one task the app should support, and the data sources it should check.

workflowEn.label: Define storage
workflowEn.detail: Use D1 for structured data and R2 for user-uploaded files when needed.

workflowEn.label: Build and test
workflowEn.detail: Have Sites build the app, then test the main flow, persistence, and responsive layout.

workflowEn.label: Set access
workflowEn.detail: Keep it private during review, then choose invited users, the workspace, or anyone with the link.

checkEn: Review stored data, access permissions, and the selected audience before deploying or changing the app from private.

workflowEn.label: Set the scope
workflowEn.detail: Specify the time period, workstream, people, or channels to review.

workflowEn.label: Find requests
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT read relevant DMs, group DMs, mentions, and thread replies.

workflowEn.label: Check current status
workflowEn.detail: Read the end of each thread to skip requests already answered or handled.

workflowEn.label: Build the action queue
workflowEn.detail: Rank items by urgency and impact with evidence, a next step, and a draft reply.

checkEn: Open the source thread to confirm the item remains unresolved, then check the recipient and tone before sending any message.

workflowEn.label: Inspect the project
workflowEn.detail: Have Codex determine whether this is a new Expo app or existing project and define one core workflow.

workflowEn.label: Build to standards
workflowEn.detail: Use Expo Router and npx expo install to maintain package compatibility.

workflowEn.label: Complete the experience
workflowEn.detail: Add native-feeling navigation, forms, lists, and loading, empty, and error states.

workflowEn.label: Test the fastest path
workflowEn.detail: Start with Expo Go or a simulator; move to a dev client or EAS only when needed.

checkEn: Verify results on a device or simulator and check package compatibility before creating a dev client or production build.

workflowEn.label: Choose a repeat task
workflowEn.detail: Define the CLI's first task and its source of truth, such as API docs, an export, database, or script.

workflowEn.label: Design the commands
workflowEn.detail: Have Codex propose a narrow command surface for search, read, download, or drafting changes.

workflowEn.label: Build with a skill
workflowEn.detail: Use $cli-creator for commands and $skill-creator to document auth and approval boundaries.

workflowEn.label: Test like a user
workflowEn.detail: Install and call the CLI from another folder; check JSON, pagination, and error messages.

checkEn: Do not put secrets in the task, and ensure write commands require explicit approval before use on real data.

workflowEn.label: Define the scope
workflowEn.detail: Specify the goal, inputs, approval source, runner, and artifact that will prove the result.

workflowEn.label: Validate and normalize
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT check required fields and normalize dates, amounts, owners, and IDs without guessing.

workflowEn.label: Dry-run first
workflowEn.detail: Run a dry run when supported, then process only approved items.

workflowEn.label: Record and verify
workflowEn.detail: Log each success or failure, retry transient errors once, and summarize the evidence.

checkEn: Stop before irreversible actions or scope expansion, and have the owner review outcome evidence after the run.

workflowEn.label: Gather planning sources
workflowEn.detail: Identify planning threads, approved documents, templates, and calendar deadlines.

workflowEn.label: Define required details
workflowEn.detail: List the audience, venue, timing, owners, support, and approvals each task needs.

workflowEn.label: Create two sections
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT clearly separate attendee-facing copy from the private operating checklist.

workflowEn.label: Close open questions
workflowEn.detail: Complete the owner map, approvals, open questions, and source appendix before execution.

checkEn: Ensure public content contains no internal operational details and every date, owner, and approval has a verified source.

workflowEn.label: Choose the core outcome
workflowEn.detail: Define one task users should complete in ChatGPT and limit the app to 3-5 tools.

workflowEn.label: Design the contract
workflowEn.detail: Define tool names, metadata, inputs, outputs, and whether v1 needs a widget.

workflowEn.label: Build the core
workflowEn.detail: Have Codex scaffold the MCP server and widget using repo patterns before adding auth.

workflowEn.label: Test in ChatGPT
workflowEn.detail: Open it over HTTPS in developer mode and try direct, indirect, and negative prompts.

checkEn: Test tool payloads and conversation flow in ChatGPT; add OAuth only for user data or write actions.

workflowEn.label: Frame the process
workflowEn.detail: Define users, inputs, outputs, scope, review date, and workflow constraints.

workflowEn.label: Gather work evidence
workflowEn.detail: Attach process docs, trackers, handoff notes, tickets, KPIs, and team discussions.

workflowEn.label: Map the real process
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT compare the documented and actual process to find owners, stuck points, and missing data.

workflowEn.label: Select automation targets
workflowEn.detail: Rank repeated steps by risk, data quality, and verification cost, then draft two specs.

checkEn: Have process owners review permissions, exception paths, irreversible actions, and ownership before automation.

workflowEn.label: Gather launch inputs
workflowEn.detail: Attach approved launch plans, product notes, trackers, creative briefs, pages, and discussions.

workflowEn.label: Identify gaps
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT flag claims, assets, audiences, and destinations that are missing or awaiting review.

workflowEn.label: Draft the campaign kit
workflowEn.detail: Create email, internal announcement, social post, content plan, briefs, and status update.

workflowEn.label: Build the approval list
workflowEn.detail: Map each asset to product, legal, and brand reviews and any related staging issues.

checkEn: Verify claims against product sources, separate public copy from internal data, and get product, legal, and brand approval.

workflowEn.label: Define joining rules
workflowEn.detail: Specify the join key, duplicate rule, period definitions, targets, and required output views.

workflowEn.label: Attach source files
workflowEn.detail: Prepare exports, mapping notes, and reporting requirements while preserving the originals.

workflowEn.label: Build the workbook
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT create joins, formulas, charts, and insights with a review section for mismatches.

workflowEn.label: Test the refresh
workflowEn.detail: Simulate the next data cycle and separate repeatable steps from decisions that still need a person.

checkEn: Verify formulas, join mismatches, and assumptions against originals whenever source files or reporting rules change.

workflowEn.label: Define the week
workflowEn.detail: Specify the date range, audience, and level of detail for the report.

workflowEn.label: Identify work sources
workflowEn.detail: List the trackers, documents, calendars, and discussion channels covering that period.

workflowEn.label: Draft the summary
workflowEn.detail: Have ChatGPT summarize completed work, decisions, blockers, follow-ups, and next priorities with links.

workflowEn.label: Verify and tune
workflowEn.detail: Check key points against source discussions and adjust the length for the audience.

checkEn: Verify decisions, blockers, and follow-ups against source materials, and remove sensitive information before sharing.
